label: Heavy going
summary:
A dark day for civilians. Russia unleashed 656 drones and 73 missiles on Ukrainian cities overnight, killing at least 22 — including a mother and her children pulled from rubble in Dnipro — in one of the deadliest barrages in months, exploiting a Patriot missile shortage worsened by the Iran war. Iran is reportedly preparing to reject the US ceasefire proposal, dimming hopes of an end to a four-month conflict.

Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt hostilities on Sunday, a fragile positive, but tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox Israelis shut down the country's centre on Monday, blocking highways and setting cars on fire over military conscription. The WMO confirmed El Nino is forming with 80% probability, warning of floods, drought, and heatwaves ahead.

Markets are cautious with Brent crude near $95 as the OECD releases its latest outlook. But the day has its counterweights: a UK rewilding site reported a 900% increase in breeding birds over 20 years, China is on track to cut emissions 17% this decade from peak, and Australia's four-day work week trial succeeded — almost every firm kept it. The mood is strained: the wars are grinding harder, not softer.

drivers.headline: Russia launches 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukrainian cities, killing at least 22 including children trapped in rubble
Iran reportedly preparing to reject US ceasefire proposal as war enters fourth month
Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt hostilities under US-brokered proposal; peace talks resume in Washington
Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox Israelis block roads and trains, set cars on fire protesting military draft
WMO confirms El Nino forming with 80% probability — warns of floods, drought, and heatwaves in coming months
Brent crude holds near $95 as Iran deal hopes fade; OECD releases latest economic outlook amid war headwinds
UK rewilding site records 900% increase in breeding birds and doubling of butterfly numbers over 20 years
China on track to cut emissions 17% this decade from 2023 peak — significantly exceeding its climate pledge
Australia's four-day work week trial succeeds: almost all firms keep the shorter week after seeing equal or higher productivity
Scientists successfully treat previously 'untreatable' eye condition for the first time
